title: Peak season fulfillment has changed — What today’s retailers must do differently
description: Peak isn’t about volume anymore, it’s about adaptability. Read the full perspective.

title: The gap between modern and legacy ESPs is widening
description: If your brand is struggling to capture gains from AI, it may be your email marketing platform. Discover four aspects that separate modern from legacy platforms.

title: Adobe Acrobat as a marketing tool? New AI features help you reach every stakeholder, every time
description: Marketers know better than anyone that how you present information is just as important as the information itself.

title: ‘Google doesn’t care that it’s terrible’: Brand, agency execs air frustrations with The Trade Desk, Google’s Performance Max, Meta’s Advantage+
description: Think transparency is hard to come by in programmatic advertising? Well, get a bunch of brand and agency executives in a room, and they’ll get super transparent about how opaque the digital ad market has become.

title: Inside Expedia’s year-long partnership with mega creator IShowSpeed
description: Expedia partnered with mega creator IShowSpeed on a record-setting livestream and year-long campaign to target Gen Z audiences.

title: Modern Retail+ Research: Marketers optimize GEO strategies amid the effects of zero-click search
description: A recent Modern Retail survey found that more than one-third of brand and agency pros (37%) said their companies have seen decreases in upper-funnel search traffic as a result of AI, and 21% said their companies have seen decreases in lower-funnel search traffic as a result of AI.

title: How GameStop went from struggling retailer to eBay bidder
description: GameStop's ambitious bid to acquire eBay shows how, over the past six years, its CEO has repositioned the company from one struggling with lower physical video game software and hardware sales to one with far bigger ambitions, arguably beyond its scope.

title: #213: AI Answers - What AI Should Never Do, Enterprise Scaling, Governing AI & Navigating IT Roadblocks
description:
Leadership wants AI everywhere. IT security can't keep up. Marketing is racing ahead while legal and finance dig in. And every week brings another story of an AI agent doing something nobody told it to do. Paul Roetzer and Cathy McPhillips answer 15 listener questions on how to actually move organizations forward and where the real opportunities lie for individuals, SMBs, and enterprises right now.

00:00:00 — Intro

00:07:01 — How do you move a company out of AI policy paralysis?

00:08:54 — How should regulated, hands-on teams introduce AI?

00:12:30 — When companies are stuck, what tends to get them moving?

00:15:00 — Should IT security evolve to adopt AI quicker or should businesses slow down?

00:17:43 — What changes an AI-skeptic employee's mind?

00:21:18 — How should early-career professionals prioritize what to learn?

00:23:29 — Is there a point where you should stop learning and start building?

00:27:29 — Where do companies get stuck scaling AI across departments?

00:29:4

title: Digiday+ Research: Marketers optimize GEO strategies amid the effects of zero-click search
description: While AI-generated search results are still relatively new compared to traditional search results, marketers are deeply feeling the effects.

title: The case for and against agentic media buying
description: Agentic media buying promises a reinvention of the programmatic ecosystem, but experts are divided on whether it could help – or hinder – accountability.

title: Media Buying Briefing: Takeshi Sano is making the rounds for Dentsu — is it enough?
description: Dentsu just retained Heineken's global business, and apparently the new CEO was involved. Should he take the heat for losing Microsoft? Most say no.

title: OpenAI Campus Network: Student club interest form
description: Join the OpenAI Campus Network—connect student clubs worldwide, access AI tools, host events, and build an AI-powered campus community.

title: How enterprises are scaling AI
description: How enterprises scale AI: from early experiments to compounding impact through trust, governance, workflow design, and quality at scale.

title: Anthropic says ‘evil’ portrayals of AI were responsible for Claude’s blackmail attempts
description: Fictional portrayals of artificial intelligence can have a real effect on AI models, according to Anthropic.

title: We’re feeling cynical about xAI’s big deal with Anthropic
description: On the latest episode of the Equity podcast, we discussed what xAI's deal with Anthropic might mean for parent company SpaceX.
